methods.description: This method describes the process of sampling stream sections and collecting Megaloptera larvae across different seasons and stream orders in Southeastern Brazil.

steps.description: Seven stream sections were sampled, located in Doce River basin and São Francisco River basin, Southeastern Brazil.
steps.searchable_summary: sample stream sections in Doce and São Francisco River basins

steps.description: Megaloptera larvae were collected manually in rainy and dry seasons from 1993-1997 and 1999-2000.
steps.searchable_summary: collect Megaloptera larvae manually during specified seasons

steps.description: Samples were taken using a Surber sampler (0.250 mm mesh) with an area of 0.0625 m².
steps.searchable_summary: use Surber sampler to collect stream samples

steps.description: Additional qualitative samples were collected using hand nets (0.250 mm mesh).
steps.searchable_summary: collect qualitative samples with hand nets

methods.description: This method outlines the processing of collected specimens, including inducing regurgitation, checking for chironomids, and preserving samples.

steps.description: Five Corydalus specimens with no attached chironomids larvae and pupae were placed in vials with 10% formalin to induce regurgitation.
steps.searchable_summary: induce regurgitation in Corydalus specimens using formalin

steps.description: Abdominal gills of Corydalus larvae were checked for chironomids presence in laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: check Corydalus larvae for chironomids in lab

steps.description: Eggs were counted by direct observations.
Commensal larvae of Corynoneura attached to Corydalus bodies were sorted out, mounted in slides, identified, preserved in 70% alcohol, and deposited in the Collection of Benthic Macroinvertebrates.
steps.searchable_summary: sort, identify, and preserve Corynoneura larvae
